item.title: AI Investments Are ‘Circular Bet’ as ROI Disappoints, Bain Survey Shows
item.description: “The technology worked. The value didn’t arrive,” Bain concluded in the report.

item.title: Anthropic to Give EU’s Cybersecurity Agency Access to Mythos
item.description: Anthropic PBC is set to give the European Union’s cybersecurity agency access to Mythos, its powerful artificial intelligence tool capable of finding and exploiting vulnerabilities in computer systems.

item.title: SpaceX Listing Is Rewriting the Rules Around Blockbuster IPOs
item.description: Index providers, fund managers rush to accommodate Musk’s company

item.title: Ethiopia Bondholders to Proceed With Plan to Sue Over Revamp
item.description: A group of holders of Ethiopia’s defaulted $1 billion eurobond will continue the process of suing the government after the latest round of restructuring negotiations fell through, according to a committee representing the investors.

item.title: Atalian Creditors Agree to Take Over Firm in Debt Restructuring
item.description: A group of bondholders of La Financiere Atalian SAS is set to take control of the French company as part of a debt restructuring.

item.title: Block Trade King Simon Sadler Takes the Stand in Segantii Trial
item.description: Segantii Capital Management founder Simon Sadler chose to take the stand Monday to give evidence in the insider trading trial that has gripped trading floors and boardrooms across Hong Kong.

item.title: Vietnam Touts IP Crackdown Results After US Launches New Probe
item.description: Vietnam’s government touted the results of a sweeping crackdown on intellectual property violations after the US launched a fresh trade investigation, even as counterfeit items remain easily accessible across the country’s street markets.

item.title: South Africa Factory Sentiment Worsens as Demand Fades
item.description: South Africa’s manufacturer sentiment worsened slightly in May as demand slumped and supply disruptions due to the Iran war continued.

item.title: Valve, the Anticorporate Hero of the Games Industry, Has Its Antitrust Moment
item.description: Lawsuits in the US and the UK allege the company’s Steam store is abusing its market power. Valve disagrees.

item.title: China Leads Economic Shift to Industrial ‘Doping,’ OECD Says
item.description: Industrial subsidies are reshaping the global economy with China standing out as a provider of state support that risks harmful distortions, according to new data from the OECD.

item.title: Revolving-Door Politics Erode Peru’s Mining Appeal Ahead of Vote
item.description: Rich deposits and low costs have made Peru a mining superpower. But as Peruvians prepare to choose their ninth president in a decade, the industry is losing competitiveness just as demand for critical minerals accelerates.

item.title: A 20% Housing Drop Still Leaves Canadians Locked Out of Market
item.description: Canada’s housing market has plunged into one of its sharpest-ever corrections. Canadians say it hasn’t gone far enough.

item.title: Meituan’s Losses Narrow After Chinese Food Delivery War Cools
item.description: Meituan’s quarterly losses shrank after repeated warnings from Beijing helped cool the red-hot competition that hammered margins in China’s online commerce arena.

item.title: Pakistan Inflation Hits Two-Year High on Energy Costs
item.description: Pakistan’s inflation accelerated to a two-year high as conflict in the Middle East drove up energy import costs.

item.title: UK Factory Growth Spurt Set to Be Short-Lived, PMI Shows
item.description: UK factories grew at the fastest pace in four years as the industry sought to get ahead of price increases and supply strains triggered by the Middle East conflict, a key survey found.

item.title: Kakao Union Calls for Strike Next Week After Wage Talks Stall
item.description: Kakao Corp.’s labor union called for a four-hour strike next week, escalating a dispute with South Korea’s biggest messaging service after negotiations over compensation and job security broke down last month.

item.title: Goldman Says Hedge Funds Buy Stocks at Fastest Pace in 6 Months
item.description: Hedge funds purchased US equities at the fastest pace in six months last week, as the S&P 500 Index extended its historic winning streak, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s prime brokerage desk.

item.title: More African Nations Seen Reaching Investment Grade, AfDB Says
item.description: More African countries are likely to regain or achieve investment-grade credit ratings by next year as reforms begin to deliver results and economic growth accelerates, African Development Bank President Sidi Ould Tah said.

item.title: Zambia Creditor Group Pushes Back on $1.36 Billion Bond Buyback
item.description: A group of holders of a Zambian $1.36 billion bond due 2053 is opposing a tender offer launched by the government, saying it was conducted without negotiation and would harm investors’ interests.

item.title: EU Proposes Tax Simplification Measures in Bid to Boost Business
item.description: The European Union will propose a streamlining of rules and exemptions in the area of taxation, its latest effort to reduce bureaucracy.

item.title: Pakistan Outlines 4% Growth Target For Next Fiscal Year
item.description: Pakistan aims to grow at a slightly faster pace in the upcoming fiscal 2027 even as the crude price shock from the Middle East war clouds the outlook for the nation that continues to rely on support from the International Monetary Fund.

item.title: The Hidden Plumbing of Commodity Finance
item.description: Who pays to keep stuff moving in an age of disruptions?

item.title: Odd Lots: The Hidden Plumbing of Commodity Finance (Podcast)
item.description: We talk about the commodity supply chain all the time. We talk about the ports and the trucks and the ships and all of that. But there’s another dimension to moving commodities all around the world, which is actually paying for it. Who funds the oil tanker and what happens when that tanker is, say, stuck in the Strait of Hormuz? Commodity finance underpins production, transportation and storage of a wide variety of the things that make the modern world, but you tend to only hear about it when th

item.title: ECB Says Consumer Price Expectations Ease But Stay Elevated
item.description: Euro-area consumers’ expectations for inflation in three years fell slightly in April, offering some relief to the European Central Bank, though policymakers are still likely to raise interest rates next week.

item.title: Germany’s Manufacturers Eked Out Slight Expansion in May
item.description: Activity in Germany’s key manufacturing sector rose slightly in May, contrasting with an initial reading that showed a small decline.

item.title: BP Dilutes Stake in Australia’s Browse With 5% Sale to GS Energy
item.description: BP Plc has agreed to dilute its stake in the A$48.7 billion Browse natural gas project by selling 5% to South Korea’s GS Energy Corp.

item.title: Adcock Faces Possible Antitrust Charges in South Africa
item.description: South Africa’s antitrust watchdog has accused pharmaceutical company Adcock Ingram Holdings Ltd. of profiteering from the Covid-19 pandemic, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Morocco Pips South Africa as Continent’s Top Industrial Economy
item.description: Morocco overtook South Africa as the continent’s most industrialized economy last year as it upgrades its offering, diversifies exports and implements growth policies, a report showed.

item.title: Turkey’s Economy Cools More Than Expected in First Quarter
item.description: Turkey’s economy cooled in the first quarter of this year as the central bank moved to tighten monetary policy to deter risks related to the Iran war.

item.title: Swiss Economy Grew Less Than Estimated at Start of the Year
item.description: Switzerland’s economy grew slightly less than initially reported in the first quarter, weighed down by stalling consumer demand and a drop in investment.
